name: 50 · Legacy File Crawl → Register by reference (file-ops crawl → log_artifact(upload=False) fills catalogue + inventory)
description:
End-to-end demo of the file-ops `crawl` operation and by-reference registration (docs/32 — the legacy file-migration capability). Nothing touches a real NAS: the first step SEEDS a small synthetic file tree on local disk, then the workflow crawls it, folds the streamed listing, and REGISTERS each file BY REFERENCE — coupling each physical copy to a logical catalogue entry without moving any bytes.

  Start{file_server_id} → seed_nas (write ~7 files into /tmp/mekhan-crawl-demo) → crawl (file-ops `crawl`: recursive streaming walk, emits `{path,size,mtime}` batches over the `crawl` control channel) → fold (consumer-edge `join: gather` flattens the batches) → register (for each file `log_artifact(..., upload=False)` — fills BOTH the catalogue AND inventory halves server-side, by reference) → End.

The `crawl` op is `list`'s recursive, streaming, checkpointable sibling, built for the ~4M-file legacy corpus: it never buffers the whole listing, `stat()`s each entry for size+mtime (the OpenDAL `fs` lister omits them), and emits fixed-size `item` batches over the job EventStream — the same docs/25 consumer-join mechanism the streaming demos use. Here `batch_size: 3`, so the ~7 seeded files arrive as multiple batches; the `crawl → fold` edge carries `join: gather`, so the compiler's gather barrier re-orders and parks the whole collection as `{output: [<batch>, …]}`, which `fold` flattens to a flat `{path,size}` list.

The load-bearing idea is by-reference registration via `log_artifact(..., upload=False)`. There is no `index` node and no urllib HTTP: a single `log_artifact` call per file IS the whole registration. The runner is co-located with the files, so the SDK/executor hashes each file locally over IPC and uploads NOTHING — the bytes stay on the file server. The platform's causality projector then fills BOTH halves of the equation atomically, server-side: a content-addressed `catalogue_entries` row (logical identity, keyed on the SHA-256) AND a physical `file_inventory` copy at `(file_server_id, reference_path)`. All 7 files have distinct content, so `files_registered` is 7. The rows then show up in the app's Inventory + Catalogue views.

Run it against your dev stack: `mekhan run demos/50-legacy-crawl-register --server http://localhost:13100 --input start.file_server_id=demo-nas` (point `--server` at your slot's mekhan port — e.g. 20100 for slot 1). It is self-contained (stdlib only, no pip installs) so it completes well within the `mekhan test` cap; registration is by reference over IPC (no HTTP), so the executor only needs to be co-located with the seeded directory.

NOTE — scale: this gather-the-stream shape is the SMALL-crawl pedagogy (the whole listing becomes one engine token). For big crawls (the real 4M-file corpus), copy demo 55-crawl-campaign instead: a cursor-loop campaign whose crawl chunks publish batches durably to the INVENTORY_FOLD stream (sink mode) and never grow an engine token.
